name: QwenPaw System Architecture
description: Intent architecture for QwenPaw covering the current platform slices plus the strengthened P0 local security audit foundation required for end-to-end non-repudiation, tamper-evident auditing, external checkpoint anchoring, out-of-band configuration tamper detection, tiered audit durability, replay-resistant cloud reconciliation, and behavior-level anomaly detection. The security extension is scoped from the AgentScope security infrastructure PRD and is intentionally limited to the minimum business closure needed for four core business scenarios and three deep-risk scenarios: full-chain evidence continuity, audit-integrity self-healing lock mode, bounded TTFT impact under full audit mode, lease-expiry-driven active defense, prompt-injection-resistant tool enforcement, stolen-session replay blocking, and silent exfiltration pattern alerting.

elements.name: QwenPaw Platform
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform
elements.description:
Repository-backed personal AI assistant platform spanning backend runtime, local operator CLI, operator console, documentation website, architecture validation assets, and the P0 security audit foundation required for local safe operation.
#file:README.md
#file:pyproject.toml
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Backend Runtime
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime
elements.description:
Python runtime root that owns API routers, agent execution, channels, config, security, providers, and shared utilities.
#file:src/qwenpaw
#file:tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py
#file:tests/unit/routers/test_git.py

elements.name: Local Security Audit Foundation
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation
elements.description:
Backend-owned P0 security foundation that standardizes implicit SecurityContext propagation, defines the canonical AuditEvent envelope, persists append-only local audit records with a hash chain, anchors continuity through a restricted checkpoint store, verifies startup consistency against configuration hashes, enforces security lock mode after integrity failures, supports human-in-the-loop confirmation for high-risk tool execution, resists prompt-driven guard bypass at the tool-call layer, and supports tiered audit durability for critical versus informational events. This element is intentionally limited to the minimum closure needed for end-to-end non-repudiation evidence chains, audit-integrity self-healing lock mode, bounded TTFT impact, active liveness defense, replay-resistant session continuity, and behavior-anomaly evidence generation rather than the full later audit pipeline.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/auth.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/agent_context.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/runner/utils.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/routers/config.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/routers/settings.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/security

attributes.name: verification_focus
attributes.value: end-to-end actor trace continuity, tamper-evident and self-healing audit integrity, accountable security configuration changes, prompt-injection-resistant high-risk enforcement, replay-resistant session continuity, behavior-level anomaly surfacing, and bounded full-audit latency

attributes.name: scope_limit
attributes.value: P0 covers implicit context propagation via contextvars, local append-only audit write, checkpoint-anchored hash continuity, configuration-hash consistency verification, security lock mode, basic query, approval persistence, human-in-the-loop confirmation records for high-risk tool execution, tool-layer enforcement against prompt-driven bypass attempts, tiered L1/L2 audit write semantics, asynchronous uplink of critical audit anchors to the cloud situational awareness platform for remote integrity mirroring, and cloud-visible anomaly evidence for replay or silent exfiltration patterns; session encryption remains later phases.

attributes.name: context_propagation_rule
attributes.value: SecurityContext must not be architecture-visible as an explicit function parameter; request entry and message dispatch must rely on implicit contextvars capture and restoration.

attributes.name: checkpoint_rule
attributes.value: A restricted append-only checkpoint store must anchor latest anchored_event_id and global_hash every N events and on each high-risk configuration change so tail truncation is detectable on restart.

attributes.name: lock_mode_rule
attributes.value: If startup consistency verification detects settings or audit-anchor divergence, backend runtime enters security lock mode and refuses sensitive tool loading until trust is re-established.

attributes.name: audit_tiering_rule
attributes.value: L1 critical events use synchronous durable write plus hash-chain and checkpoint updates before returning; L2 informational reasoning events may batch asynchronously and remain visible as pending verification until anchored.

attributes.name: uplink_rule
attributes.value: Critical audit anchors and L1 audit records must be asynchronously uplinked with retry to a cloud integrity mirror; before sending new data after reconnect, the client must perform a pre-upload hash-alignment handshake and refuse sync if local and cloud anchors diverge.

attributes.name: high_risk_confirmation_rule
attributes.value: High-risk tool execution must pause for explicit user confirmation, persist the confirmation artifact, and emit a synchronous L1 USER_CONFIRMATION audit record before execution resumes.

attributes.name: tool_layer_enforcement_rule
attributes.value: High-risk enforcement must execute at the tool invocation boundary rather than trusting conversational intent classification; prompts that claim test mode, maintenance mode, or other bypass narratives cannot suppress guard evaluation when the tool risk remains High.

attributes.name: session_replay_rule
attributes.value: Cloud-side reconciliation must validate that each trusted trace or session advances through the expected monotonic sequence and hash continuity. A replayed or cloned session presented from an unaudited process must be blocked before additional actions are authorized.

attributes.name: trust_recovery_rule
attributes.value: Whenever a client resumes from UNTRUSTED state, it must perform a mandatory full-chain sync for the missing sequence gap. No new high-risk tools are permitted until the gap is validated by the cloud mirror.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-024-end-to-end-non-repudiation-evidence-chain
testcases.description: When an employee triggers a high-risk action through a third-party agent plugin, the system must preserve a complete business-visible evidence chain from the original user to the final high-risk tool effect and bind that chain to the user's confirmation artifact.
testcases.Input: Authenticate as user A, ask QwenPaw to delegate a high-risk operation through Agent B and third-party plugin C toward high-risk tool D, then provide the required user confirmation so the action is allowed to continue.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center and the audit query surface expose a continuous evidence chain from user A to Agent B to plugin C to tool D together with the confirmation artifact digest, and no link in the chain is anonymous, broken, or attributed to a fallback system identity.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-025-audit-integrity-self-healing-lockdown
testcases.description: When an employee attempts to hide a sensitive action by deleting records, editing configuration files, or reconnecting after offline tampering, the system must surface a single business-level data continuity anomaly and refuse further sensitive use until trust is re-established.
testcases.Input: Execute a sensitive action, unplug the network cable, modify or truncate the local audit store or settings database by operating-system means, restart the client, then attempt to reconnect or invoke another sensitive tool.
testcases.TestResults: Before the device can continue sensitive work or upload new anchors, the system raises a data continuity anomaly in Security Center, enters local lock mode, and refuses sensitive tools until the local ledger, checkpoint anchors, and cloud mirror are reconciled.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-027-lease-expiry-active-defense
testcases.description: When a QwenPaw device goes missing or stays offline beyond its lease window, Security Center must actively downgrade the device to untrusted and keep it blocked until it proves continuity on return.
testcases.Input: Let a client miss its heartbeat long enough to exceed the lease TTL, observe the cloud dashboard state turn untrusted, then reconnect the device and attempt to resume model access without first completing the missing-gap verification.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center marks the device untrusted when the lease expires, denies restored authorization on reconnect until the full missing hash gap is validated, and only then allows the node to regain normal model access.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-021-prompt-injection-tool-guard-enforced
testcases.description: A malicious prompt or contaminated external text must not be able to talk an agent into skipping the high-risk confirmation path or into treating a high-risk action as if it were a trusted test operation.
testcases.Input: In a live conversation, submit nested or deceptive instructions that claim the agent is in test mode or otherwise exempt from approval, then drive the agent toward a tool whose configured risk level is High without providing a valid trusted context or confirmation artifact.
testcases.TestResults: The high-risk tool remains blocked at the tool-call layer, the guard rejects execution because the required trusted context and confirmation are absent, and the attempted bypass is recorded as a rejected security-relevant event rather than silently executing.

testcases.name: sec-e2e-022-session-hijack-replay-blocked
testcases.description: If an attacker steals a still-valid session context and replays it from an unaudited process, Security Center must detect that the session lineage no longer matches the next expected sequence and stop the session before it can continue operating.
testcases.Input: Capture a valid session context after an authenticated operation, inject it into a second process that is not participating in the audited runtime, and attempt to emit additional operations under the same trace or session identity.
testcases.TestResults: Security Center or its cloud verifier detects that the replayed trace or session does not match the next expected sequence and hash continuity, blocks further authorization for that session, and surfaces the attempted hijack as a business-visible alert.

elements.name: Implicit Security Context Manager
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Implicit Security Context Manager
elements.description:
Runtime manager that captures SecurityContext in FastAPI middleware, stores it in Python contextvars, packages it into AgentScope message metadata when crossing asynchronous message boundaries, and restores it on the consumer side so lower-layer tools can resolve identity implicitly. Explicit SecurityContext parameter threading is forbidden by this intent element.
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/auth.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/agent_context.py
#file:src/qwenpaw/app/runner/utils.py

elements.name: Security Context
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Security Context
elements.description:
Canonical runtime context carried across request entry, agent execution, tool execution, and security-configuration mutation so that audit and policy decisions share one stable identity envelope. The minimum P0 payload includes trace_id, user_id, session_id, root_session_id, agent_id, channel, the acted-on security scope when present, and enough trusted provenance metadata to distinguish audited runtime continuity from replayed or cloned session usage.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

attributes.name: integrity_binding
attributes.description: Opaque provenance binding or signature reference that lets downstream guards verify the context originated from a trusted audited runtime rather than an injected or replayed process.

elements.name: Audit Event
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Event
elements.description:
Canonical append-only security audit record that captures event identity, event type, actor, session, agent, resource or configuration scope, decision or result metadata, prior_hash, current_hash, and sufficient fields to support integrity verification, remote integrity mirroring, replay detection, and basic operator query.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

attributes.name: sync_status
attributes.description: Per-entry uplink state for DEMO integrity mirroring. Valid values are PENDING, SYNCED, and FAILED.

attributes.name: sequence_number
attributes.description: Monotonic sequence ordinal used by the local verifier and cloud mirror to detect missing, duplicated, or replayed operations within a trusted trace or session.

attributes.name: confirmation_signature
attributes.description: Optional user confirmation signature persisted on audit entries that record explicit approval for high-risk actions.

attributes.name: auth_token_ref
attributes.description: Optional reference to the confirmation token or authentication artifact that authorized a high-risk action without embedding the raw secret in the audit payload.

attributes.name: resource_access_summary
attributes.description: Optional summary of resource category, access frequency, and aggregation scope used by cloud-side behavior analytics to distinguish normal work from silent exfiltration patterns.

elements.name: Audit Checkpoint Anchor
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Checkpoint Anchor
elements.description:
Restricted checkpoint record stored outside the primary audit-event log so tail truncation becomes detectable. The minimum P0 payload includes anchored_event_id, global_hash, checkpoint_reason, checkpoint_timestamp, and the protected storage location metadata used during startup integrity verification.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Tiered Audit Stream
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Tiered Audit Stream
elements.description:
Audit write orchestration that separates L1 critical events from L2 informational reasoning events. L1 uses synchronous durable writes and checkpoint updates before sensitive actions continue, while L2 batches asynchronously and remains operator-visible as pending verification until anchored.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: High Risk Tool Guard
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/High Risk Tool Guard
elements.description:
Interceptor that evaluates every tool invocation against the configured risk policy at the tool-call boundary. Whenever an agent attempts to invoke a tool marked with High risk level, it pauses execution, requires trusted Security Context provenance plus a user-provided confirmation token or signature, records the confirmation as a synchronous L1 USER_CONFIRMATION audit event, and only then releases the blocked tool execution path. Prompt content, nested instructions, or claimed test-mode narratives cannot bypass this enforcement.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Audit Uplink Manager
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Manager
elements.description:
Manager responsible for asynchronously pushing the local ledger tail state, including last event id and global hash, plus L1 critical audit records to the cloud situational awareness platform. The DEMO path uses fire-and-forget with retries, supports resume after reconnect, and must perform a pre-upload handshake against the cloud mirror before sending new data.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Heartbeat Emitter
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Manager/Heartbeat Emitter
elements.description:
Emitter that periodically reads the most recent EventID and GlobalHash from the local audit ledger and sends them, together with client identity proof, to the Security Center as a lease heartbeat. The DEMO cadence is periodic, for example every five minutes.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Audit Uplink Service
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Local Security Audit Foundation/Audit Uplink Service
elements.description:
Edge-side transport adapter that implements secure audit uplink. Whenever an L1 audit record or critical anchor is durably written, it triggers an asynchronous HTTPS or gRPC call that sends an encrypted audit summary toward the Security Center while preserving retry and resume semantics under intermittent connectivity.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Security Schema Contract
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Backend Runtime/Security Schema Contract
elements.description:
Shared contract that fixes the hash algorithm, canonical JSON field ordering, and serialization rules used by both edge-side uplink and cloud-side verification. Its purpose is to prevent false integrity failures caused by formatting drift rather than real tampering.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Security Center
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center
elements.description:
Security hub deployed in a controlled environment such as cloud or server infrastructure. It aggregates audit uplinks from multiple QwenPaw nodes, maintains per-client shadow audit chains, reconstructs end-to-end evidence chains for operator review, triggers real-time data continuity alerts whenever incoming integrity state diverges from the stored cloud expectation, blocks replayed session lineages, and surfaces abnormal behavior patterns such as covert low-risk data sweeps in a business-facing visual console.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Audit Lease Monitor
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Audit Lease Monitor
elements.description:
Monitor that maintains the lease registry for all connected clients. It applies a TTL threshold, for example thirty minutes, marks devices UNTRUSTED when no valid heartbeat is received within the threshold, and drives dashboard state plus forced-recovery requirements on reconnect.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

elements.name: Cloud Integrity Stub
elements.browser_path: QwenPaw Platform/Security Center/Cloud Integrity Stub
elements.description:
Logical verification stub inside the Security Center used for the DEMO. It receives critical audit anchors and L1 events from one QwenPaw client, maintains a shadow hash chain for that client, validates each uplink by comparing incoming prior_hash against the cloud-stored current_hash, and rejects replayed or cloned session activity when the presented trace lineage does not match the next expected sequence.
#file:prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md

attributes.name: external_scope
attributes.value: Cloud-side verifier modeled as an external application-side collaboration participant rather than a repository-owned runtime component.

views.description: Stable platform slices and the strengthened P0 local security audit foundation required for safe local operation.

Intent view for the strengthened local security audit closure that standardizes implicit context propagation, audit event shape, checkpoint-anchored tamper evidence, out-of-band configuration consistency verification, tiered logging semantics, and edge-to-cloud integrity mirroring with pre-upload handshake.
Cross-boundary view showing how edge-side audit capture, uplink transport, shared canonical schema rules, and cloud-side mirroring combine into one security collaboration loop.
